title: Berlin Then and Now - Quick Summary
items: **Compare evidence, not pixels.** Historic and current photographs rarely share the same position or lens, so a perfect visual overlay can be convincing and still be wrong.
**Start with one surviving anchor.** At Mehringplatz, the Friedenssäule marks the centre across the oldest and newest views.
**Read the street geometry next.** Stand south of the Peace Column, face north and follow the opening toward Friedrichstraße to understand the inherited axis.
**Treat missing space as evidence.** The 1957 photograph shows the cleared square between destruction and the postwar plan; that middle stage explains more than a simple before-and-after pair.
**Mehringplatz is a translation, not a replica.** The postwar housing scheme kept the circular idea while changing its dimensions, traffic pattern and architecture.
**Use the same method elsewhere in Berlin.** Check one fixed object, the road directions, the scale of the gap and whether an old-looking element was rebuilt or moved.
